page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals that the m6gd.large delivers moderate single-core CPU performance but exhibits limitations under multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance scored 1519.39, placing it in the average tier, whereas multi-core performance dropped to the weak tier at 3013.17. Memory bandwidth tests showed 117.17 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 38.91 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, both falling into the poor to weak categories. Similarly, the Geekbench multi-core score of 1752.0 and Passmark CPU score of 1344.38 reflect lower-tier compute capabilities, with software compilation and image processing benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6gd.large leverages the ARM64 architecture to offer a lower-cost alternative to x86 instances for workloads that do not require high-performance computing or extensive memory bandwidth. The primary tradeoff is the lower multi-threaded performance and lack of advanced vector extensions such as AVX or AMX. However, the inclusion of a fast 118 GB local NVMe SSD provides high-speed local storage that benefits applications requiring temporary scratch space. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and applications that can run efficiently on ARM-based processors without demanding high memory throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 118 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.39, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower tiers, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1752.0. The instance lacks advanced vector extensions but includes hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. This configuration offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring local NVMe storage and moderate compute power, making it suitable for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and general-purpose ARM64 applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, delivering cost-efficient performance for lightweight database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB NVMe local storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, integrated NVMe local storage, and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 118 GB local NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers average single-core CPU performance but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth
Features hardware-accelerated AES cryptography on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and general-purpose ARM applications
